api.title: 🌐 API Simulation
api.subtitle: Practice API testing with different HTTP status codes
api.description: Simulate different API responses by clicking the buttons below. Check the status code and response message.
api.booksDescription: Full CRUD operations for managing a digital library.

postmanSteps.step5: 5. Go to Body > raw > JSON and paste the request body (if needed)

getBooks.description: Retrieves a list of all available books in the library.

getBookById.description: Retrieves a specific book using its unique ID.

createBook.description: Adds a new book to the library.

updateBook.description: Updates an existing book's information by ID.

deleteBook.description: Removes a book from the library permanently.

comparison.title: 🔍 Cypress, Selenium, and Playwright Command Comparison
comparison.conclusionText: Each tool has its own strengths:
comparison.seleniumStrength: Mature, flexible, wide language and browser support
comparison.playwrightStrength: Modern, fast, powerful API and network control
comparison.finalAdvice: Choose the most suitable tool according to your project requirements, team skills, and test scope.

locator.title: 🎯 Selenium vs Playwright Locator Guide
locator.subtitle: Migration from Java Selenium to TypeScript Playwright

headers.playwrightOnlyFeatures: 🎭 The following features are only available in Playwright!

tips.comp1: CSS selectors are default in Playwright. Use # for ID.
tips.comp3: Use square brackets for Attribute selectors.
tips.comp4: Role-based selectors in Playwright are more accessible and reliable.
tips.comp5: Using getByRole is faster and more readable than XPath.
tips.comp6: CSS selectors work the same way in both frameworks.
tips.comp7: Playwright's getByPlaceholder method is much more practical.
tips.comp8: getByLabel is much cleaner than complex XPaths.
tips.comp9: Using role-based selectors for headings is best practice.
tips.comp10: Test IDs are the most reliable selectors in test environments.
tips.comp11: In Playwright, locator captures multiple elements automatically.
tips.comp12: You can do partial text search with getByText.
tips.comp13: getByAltText is the best option for images.
tips.comp14: Chained selectors are cleaner in Playwright.
tips.comp15: Using role for checkboxes is a more semantic approach.
tips.comp16: You can use selectOption() method for dropdowns.
tips.comp17: getByRole('cell') can also be used for tables.
tips.comp18: getByTitle is very practical for title attributes.
tips.comp19: Locator chaining is more readable in Playwright.
tips.comp20: Combining with dot is the same for multiple classes.
tips.pw1: You can select the first of multiple elements with first().
tips.pw2: last() selects the last element in the list.
tips.pw3: You can select the element at a specific index with nth(index). Index starts from 0.
tips.pw4: You can filter elements by text or other criteria with filter().
tips.pw5: You can find a parent containing a specific element with has.
tips.pw6: You can check element state with Playwright assertion methods.
tips.pw7: You can easily check element states (enabled/disabled).
tips.pw8: Special methods are available for checkbox operations.
tips.pw9: toHaveText checks exact match, toContainText checks partial match.
tips.pw10: Checking or getting input values is very easy.
tips.pw11: You can check class existence with string or regex.
tips.pw12: You can check any HTML attribute or get its value.
tips.pw13: You can check or get the count of found elements.
tips.pw14: You can easily check link href and page URL.
tips.pw15: Playwright automatically waits for elements to be ready!
tips.pw16: You can make more specific selections by chaining locators.
tips.pw17: You can select from dropdown in 3 different ways.
tips.pw18: You can easily access elements inside iframe with frameLocator.
tips.pw19: You can get all elements as array with all() and loop through them.
tips.pw20: You can upload files even to hidden file inputs.

jmeter.title: ⚡ Apache JMeter
jmeter.subtitle: Performance & Load Testing Tool
jmeter.intro: Apache JMeter is a free, open-source Java application designed to load test and measure the performance of web applications, APIs, and services.
jmeter.whatIsDesc: JMeter simulates heavy load on a server to test its strength and analyze overall performance under different load types. It is widely used for performance, load, stress, and functional testing.

whyUseItems.protocols: Supports HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, SOAP, REST, JDBC and more

jmeter.installationDesc: Download JMeter from apache.jmeter.net. Requires Java 8 or higher.

conceptsItems.config: Config Elements — CSV Data Set, HTTP Defaults

jmeter.firstTestDesc: Step-by-step guide: Create Thread Group → Add HTTP Sampler → Add Listener → Configure & Run.
jmeter.reportsDesc: Generate rich HTML reports with charts for Response Time, Throughput, Error Rate, Percentiles and more.

sql.title: 🗄️ SQL
sql.subtitle: Database Testing & Query Mastery
sql.intro: SQL (Structured Query Language) is the standard language for managing relational databases and is essential for backend and database testing in automation.
sql.whatIsDesc: SQL is a domain-specific language for managing data held in relational databases. It enables creating, reading, updating, and deleting records (CRUD).

whyUseItems.verify: Verify database state after UI/API actions
whyUseItems.seed: Seed test data before test execution
whyUseItems.cleanup: Clean up test data after tests
whyUseItems.direct: Directly query backend to validate business logic

sql.selectDesc: Retrieve data with SELECT, FROM, WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT.
sql.joinsDesc: INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, FULL JOIN — combining data from multiple tables.
sql.aggregatesDesc: COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX with GROUP BY and HAVING clauses.
sql.subqueriesDesc: Nested queries and Common Table Expressions for complex data retrieval.
sql.dmlDesc: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE — modifying database records for test setup and teardown.
sql.testingDesc: Schema validation, referential integrity, stored procedure testing, and data migration verification.

typescript.title: 📘 TypeScript
typescript.subtitle: Typed JavaScript for Reliable Automation
typescript.intro: TypeScript is a strongly typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JS. It brings type safety, better tooling, and improved maintainability to your automation framework.
typescript.whatIsDesc: TypeScript adds optional static types to JavaScript. Catch bugs at compile time, get better IDE support, and write more maintainable automation code.

whyUseItems.oop: Full OOP support: interfaces, generics, decorators

typescript.basicsDesc: Types, interfaces, enums, type aliases, union types, intersection types.
typescript.classesDesc: Object-oriented patterns for Page Object Model, base classes, and service abstractions.
typescript.genericsDesc: Write reusable, type-safe utility functions and helper classes.
typescript.asyncDesc: Modern async patterns essential for Playwright automation.
typescript.playwrightDesc: Playwright was built TypeScript-first. Leverage full type safety for page interactions, fixtures, and assertions.
typescript.configDesc: Compiler options, module resolution, strict mode settings for automation projects.

python.title: 🐍 Python
python.subtitle: Versatile Automation with Python
python.intro: Python is one of the most popular languages for test automation. Its clear syntax, rich libraries (pytest, Selenium, Requests), and large community make it ideal for all automation needs.
python.whatIsDesc: Python's simplicity, readability, and extensive library ecosystem make it perfect for UI automation, API testing, data-driven testing, and CI/CD integration.

whyUseItems.simple: Simple, readable syntax — easy to learn
whyUseItems.pytest: pytest — powerful and extensible testing framework
whyUseItems.selenium: Selenium WebDriver support out of the box

python.pytestDesc: Fixtures, parametrize, markers, plugins — the gold standard for Python testing.
python.seleniumDesc: Browser automation with Selenium WebDriver, Page Object Model, and wait strategies.
python.requestsDesc: HTTP requests for API testing: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE with headers and auth.
python.datadrivenDesc: Parametrize tests with CSV, JSON, Excel data using pytest.mark.parametrize.
python.pomDesc: Design pattern for maintainable Selenium automation with Python.
python.bestpracticesDesc: Project structure, fixtures, conftest.py, environment management with .env files.

practice.title: 🛠️ Practice Playground
practice.subtitle: Practice automation with modern UI elements
